fixture_purpose: Cross-version benchmark — v2 must reproduce v1's reading on the same corpus within ±0.10 of Γ. The two-cluster severed-edge topology must persist.
fixture_type: benchmark (not a constructed test text — actual v1 output)

prompt_versions_locked.note: These prompts apply if v2 corpus mode is run with Argument Mode at corpus scale (not v2.0 default — Argument Mode is per-document in v2.0 per D-10). For v2.0, this benchmark applies only to Topology Mode at corpus scale, where the comparison is against v1's heuristic Γ → v2's Γ_spec on embedding-based concept graph.

gamma_spec.tolerance_note: v2 spectral Γ should fall within ±0.10 of v1 heuristic Γ (0.605) on the same corpus. If outside this range, the disagreement IS informative — investigate before proceeding.

psi_or_cavity_score.rationale: v1 Ψ = 2.253 was the heuristic blend. v2 Topology Mode does not directly compute Ψ; it computes Γ_dirichlet. Argument-mode-on-corpus would compute cavity_score, but corpus-Argument-mode is post-MVP per D-10.

expected_partition_signature: large severed cluster centered on: draken, structural, signal, framework, model, social, human, structure
smaller severed cluster centered on: clinch, restriction, capacity, global, time, maintain, power
structural_signature.calibration_note: If v2's Fiedler cut produces a cleanly different partition (e.g., single large cluster, or three-cluster structure), the embedding-based concept graph is identifying different latent topology than v1's keyword-co-occurrence graph. This may be an improvement but requires investigation. A different cut is acceptable; cut absence is not.

layer_coverage_signature.calibration_note: v1's top-10 salience: system, draken, structural, signal, coherence, framework, model, layer, narrative, institutional. v2 must reproduce ≥ 7 of these 10 (allowing for embedding-based ranking to surface adjacent terms). Drop below 7 = different framework being measured.

discipline_notes: This is the only fixture for which v1 and v2 are expected to substantially agree. Disagreement is not a failure but is a research finding that must be analyzed before continuing.

If v2 produces Γ_spec ≥ 0.85 (substantially higher than v1), the embedding-based geometry has smoothed over distinctions v1's keyword tagging caught. Investigate whether v1 was over-flagging severance, or v2 is under-flagging.

If v2 produces Γ_spec ≤ 0.30, v2's spectral computation is treating embedding geometry as severance where v1 found gluing. Likely the restriction-map identity-strategy is too lenient at corpus scale — consider switching to type-conditioned (D-3 strategy 2) earlier than planned.
Either disagreement justifies a paragraph in the v2 launch post explaining the discrepancy, since the framework's coherence requires the two analyzers' diagnoses to be commensurable.
